reasoning: The base rate of billionaires successfully creating new political parties is incredibly low. Elon Musk often makes grand statements on X (formerly Twitter) about politics, but the required criteria — officially founding an organization with the stated purpose of running its own candidates (not merely an endorsing PAC) and tangible evidence like a dedicated website/social media presence — sets a very high bar. The market's current value is ~5.2%, reflecting appropriately high skepticism. The friction of actually setting up a third party in the US system, combined with Musk's already heavily stretched commitments across his companies and the existing DOGE commission apparatus aligned with the GOP, severely restricts the likelihood that this will materialize as a formal political party before the end of 2025.
